--   let tx = Transaction { verifier = verifier, userId = userId, expiresAt = now + 300 }
--   store.put key tx
--   
--   -- Later, in callback:
--   let key = TransactionKey.fromText stateToken
--   maybeTx &lt;- store.consume key  -- Atomically get + delete
--   </pre>

module Auth.OAuth2.TransactionStore.InMemory

-- | Create a new in-memory TransactionStore.
--   
--   WARNING: Development only. See module documentation.
--   
--   The returned store guarantees atomic <a>consume</a> semantics within a
--   single process. Multiple instances will break the one-time use
--   guarantee - use Redis for production multi-instance deployments.
--   
--   Includes a background reaper that cleans up expired transactions every
--   60 seconds.
--   
--   <pre>
--   store &lt;- TransactionStore.InMemory.new
--   </pre>
new :: Task Text TransactionStore


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter
--   
--   Simple in-memory rate limiter for OAuth2 endpoints.
--   
--   <h1>Security Purpose</h1>
--   
--   Rate limiting prevents abuse of OAuth2 endpoints:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>/connect</tt> - Prevents attackers from generating excessive
--   state tokens</li>
--   <li><tt>/callback</tt> - Prevents brute-force attacks on state
--   validation</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Algorithm</h1>
--   
--   Uses a sliding window counter per key (IP address, userId, or
--   provider). Requests are counted within a configurable time window.
--   
--   <h1>Limitations</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>In-memory only - does not work across multiple instances</li>
--   <li>No persistence - limits reset on restart</li>
--   <li>For production with multiple instances, use Redis</li>
--   </ul>

module Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter

-- | Rate limiter interface.
data RateLimiter
RateLimiter :: (Text -> Task Text RateLimitResult) -> RateLimiter

-- | Check if a request should be rate limited. Takes a key (e.g., IP
--   address, userId) and returns the result.
[checkLimit] :: RateLimiter -> Text -> Task Text RateLimitResult

-- | Rate limit configuration.
data RateLimitConfig
RateLimitConfig :: Int -> Int -> RateLimitConfig

-- | Maximum requests allowed in the window
[maxRequests] :: RateLimitConfig -> Int

-- | Time window in seconds
[windowSeconds] :: RateLimitConfig -> Int

-- | Result of a rate limit check.
data RateLimitResult

-- | Request is allowed
Allowed :: RateLimitResult

-- | Request is rate limited (retry after N seconds)
Limited :: Int -> RateLimitResult

-- | Create a new rate limiter with the given configuration.
--   
--   Includes a background reaper that cleans up old buckets every 5
--   minutes.
new :: RateLimitConfig -> Task Text RateLimiter

-- | Default rate limit for /connect endpoint.
--   
--   10 requests per minute per key (userId). This is generous for normal
--   use but limits abuse.
defaultConnectConfig :: RateLimitConfig

-- | Default rate limit for /callback endpoint.
--   
--   20 requests per minute per key (IP address). Higher limit because
--   legitimate callbacks may retry.
defaultCallbackConfig :: RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitResult
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RequestBucket
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitResult
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RequestBucket
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitConfig
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RateLimitResult
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.RateLimiter.RequestBucket


-- | # Auth.OAuth2.Routes
--   
--   HTTP route handlers for OAuth2 provider integration.
--   
--   <h1>Routes</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>GET <i>connect</i>{provider}</tt> - Initiate OAuth2 flow
--   (requires JWT)</li>
--   <li><tt>GET <i>callback</i>{provider}</tt> - OAuth2 callback (no JWT,
--   validates state)</li>
--   <li><tt>POST <i>disconnect</i>{provider}</tt> - Disconnect provider
--   (requires JWT)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Security</h1>
--   
--   The connect and disconnect routes require JWT authentication because
--   they need the userId to:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Store userId server-side in TransactionStore (connect) - GDPR
--   compliant</li>
--   <li>Delete tokens for the correct user (disconnect)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   The callback route does NOT require JWT because:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>User is redirected back from external OAuth2 provider</li>
--   <li>The state token (HMAC-signed) identifies the transaction</li>
--   <li>userId is retrieved from server-side TransactionStore (NOT from
--   token)</li>
--   <li>State is validated and consumed atomically (one-time use)</li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   <h1>GDPR Compliance</h1>
--   
--   NO PII (userId, email, etc.) is stored in the state token. The state
--   token only contains: provider name, cryptographic nonce, and
--   timestamps. userId is stored server-side in TransactionStore and
--   retrieved at callback.
--   
--   <h1>Flow</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   1. User clicks "Connect Oura" button
--   2. Frontend calls GET <i>connect</i>oura (with JWT)
--   3. Server generates state token (provider + nonce), stores verifier + userId
--   4. Server redirects to Oura authorization URL
--   5. User authorizes on Oura
--   6. Oura redirects to GET <i>callback</i>oura?code=...&amp;state=...
--   7. Server validates state, retrieves userId from TransactionStore
--   8. Server exchanges code for tokens, calls onSuccess callback
--   9. Server redirects user to successRedirectUrl
--   </pre>

module Auth.OAuth2.Routes

-- | OAuth2 route handlers.
--   
--   These are WAI-independent handlers that can be integrated into
--   WebTransport.
data OAuth2Routes
OAuth2Routes :: (Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError Text) -> (Text -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError (Text, Maybe OAuth2Action)) -> (Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError OAuth2Action) -> OAuth2Routes

-- | Handle GET <i>connect</i>{provider} Returns: Redirect URL to OAuth2
--   provider
[handleConnect] :: OAuth2Routes -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError Text

-- | Handle GET <i>callback</i>{provider}?code=...&amp;state=... Returns:
--   (redirect URL, maybe action to dispatch)
[handleCallback] :: OAuth2Routes -> Text -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError (Text, Maybe OAuth2Action)

-- | Handle POST <i>disconnect</i>{provider} Returns: Action to dispatch
[handleDisconnect] :: OAuth2Routes -> Text -> Text -> Task OAuth2RouteError OAuth2Action

-- | Create OAuth2 route handlers with the given dependencies.
createRoutes :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> OAuth2Routes

-- | Dependencies required by OAuth2 routes.
--   
--   These are injected at route creation time.
data OAuth2RouteDeps
OAuth2RouteDeps :: HmacKey -> TransactionStore -> SecretStore -> Map Text ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig -> RateLimiter -> RateLimiter -> OAuth2RouteDeps

-- | HMAC key for signing/verifying state tokens
[hmacKey] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> HmacKey

-- | Store for PKCE verifiers (one-time use)
[transactionStore] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> TransactionStore

-- | Secure storage for OAuth2 tokens
[secretStore] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> SecretStore

-- | Configured OAuth2 providers by name (pre-validated at startup)
[providers] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> Map Text ValidatedOAuth2ProviderConfig

-- | Rate limiter for /connect endpoint (per userId)
[connectRateLimiter] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> RateLimiter

-- | Rate limiter for /callback endpoint (per IP, but we use state token as
--   proxy)
[callbackRateLimiter] :: OAuth2RouteDeps -> RateLimiter

-- | Errors that can occur in OAuth2 routes.
data OAuth2RouteError

-- | Provider not configured
ProviderNotFound :: Text -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | State token validation failed
StateValidationFailed :: StateTokenError -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | State not found in transaction store (expired or already used)
StateNotFound :: OAuth2RouteError

-- | OAuth2 token exchange failed
TokenExchangeFailed :: OAuth2Error -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | Provider name in state doesn't match route
ProviderMismatch :: Text -> Text -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | Missing required query parameter
MissingParameter :: Text -> OAuth2RouteError

-- | Rate limit exceeded (retry after N seconds)
RateLimited :: Int -> OAuth2RouteError
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.OAuth2.Routes.OAuth2RouteError
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.OAuth2.Routes.OAuth2RouteError
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.OAuth2.Routes.OAuth2RouteError


-- | High-performance JWKS manager for JWT authentication. Provides
--   lock-free key lookups with background refresh.
--   
--   Architecture: - Hot path (per request): AtomicVar.peek for lock-free
--   reads - Control plane (background): Periodic refresh, missing-key
--   handling
--   
--   Performance target: 50k req/s with lock-free key lookups.

module Auth.Jwks

-- | JWKS Manager with background refresh.
data JwksManager
JwksManager :: AuthConfig -> AtomicVar KeySnapshot -> AtomicVar RefreshState -> AtomicVar (Maybe (AsyncTask Text Unit)) -> AtomicVar Bool -> JwksManager

-- | Auth configuration (issuer, jwksUri, etc.)
[config] :: JwksManager -> AuthConfig

-- | Hot path: lock-free reads
[keySnapshot] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar KeySnapshot

-- | Atomic refresh state (cooldown + mutual exclusion)
[refreshState] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar RefreshState

-- | Background refresh task (if running)
[refreshTask] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar (Maybe (AsyncTask Text Unit))

-- | Whether the manager is running
[isRunning] :: JwksManager -> AtomicVar Bool

-- | Start a new JWKS manager with background refresh. Performs initial key
--   fetch before returning.
startManager :: AuthConfig -> Task Text JwksManager

-- | Stop the JWKS manager and cancel background refresh.
stopManager :: JwksManager -> Task Text Unit

-- | Look up a key by kid (key ID). This is the hot path - uses lock-free
--   reads.
lookupKey :: Text -> JwksManager -> Task err (Maybe JWK)

-- | Get all cached keys. Used when no kid is specified in the token.
getAllKeys :: JwksManager -> Task err (Array JWK)

-- | Get the cached JWKSet for signature verification. This is the
--   preferred hot path - avoids per-request JWKSet construction.
getJwkSet :: JwksManager -> Task err JWKSet

-- | Get a JWKSet containing only the key with the given kid. This is the
--   optimal path when the token contains a kid claim. Uses pre-cached
--   singleton JWKSet to avoid per-request allocation. Returns the full
--   JWKSet if kid is not found (fallback to try all keys).
getJwkSetForKid :: Text -> JwksManager -> Task err JWKSet

-- | Get JWKSet for kid, triggering refresh if kid not found. Returns
--   (JWKSet, kidWasFound). If kid not found: 1. Triggers background
--   refresh (rate-limited) 2. Re-reads snapshot to pick up newly rotated
--   keys 3. Returns full JWKSet as fallback if still not found Uses
--   pre-cached singleton JWKSet to avoid per-request allocation.
getJwkSetForKidWithRefresh :: Text -> JwksManager -> Task err (JWKSet, Bool)

-- | Check if keys are stale beyond acceptable threshold. Returns True if
--   keys should not be used (triggers 503 response). OPTIMIZED: No
--   per-request clock IO - just reads the precomputed isStale flag. The
--   isStale flag is set by the refresh loop when refresh fails.
checkStaleness :: JwksManager -> Task err Bool

-- | Request an immediate refresh (e.g., when a kid is not found). This is
--   rate-limited and uses atomic state to prevent thundering herd. Only
--   one refresh can run at a time across all callers (including background
--   refresh). Uses Task.finally to ensure lock is released even if refresh
--   throws an exception.
requestRefresh :: JwksManager -> Task err Unit

-- | Snapshot of cached keys for lock-free reads.
data KeySnapshot
KeySnapshot :: Map Text JWK -> Map Text JWKSet -> JWKSet -> Array JWK -> Int64 -> Int64 -> Bool -> KeySnapshot

-- | Current keys indexed by kid
[keysByKid] :: KeySnapshot -> Map Text JWK

-- | Pre-built singleton JWKSets per kid (avoid per-request allocation)
[jwkSetsByKid] :: KeySnapshot -> Map Text JWKSet

-- | Pre-built JWKSet with all keys (for tokens without kid)
[cachedJwkSet] :: KeySnapshot -> JWKSet

-- | Pre-built array of all keys (for tokens without kid)
[allKeysArray] :: KeySnapshot -> Array JWK

-- | Unix timestamp when keys were fetched
[fetchedAt] :: KeySnapshot -> Int64

-- | Monotonic version for stale-race prevention
[snapshotVersion] :: KeySnapshot -> Int64

-- | True if refresh failed and keys are beyond max stale time
[isStale] :: KeySnapshot -> Bool

-- | Refresh state for atomic cooldown and mutual exclusion.
data RefreshState
RefreshState :: Int64 -> Bool -> RefreshState

-- | Unix timestamp of last refresh attempt
[lastAttemptAt] :: RefreshState -> Int64

-- | True if a refresh is currently running
[refreshInProgress] :: RefreshState -> Bool
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Jwks.KeySnapshot
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Jwks.RefreshState


-- | User claims extracted from a validated JWT. This is the primary type
--   Jess interacts with - everything else is internal.

module Auth.Claims

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Claims.UserClaims
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Auth.Claims.UserClaims


-- | Query authorization types and helper combinators.
--   
--   This module provides the building blocks for declarative query
--   authorization. Queries define two functions:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>canAccess</tt>: Checked before fetching data. "Can this user
--   access this query type at all?"</li>
--   <li><tt>canView</tt>: Checked after fetching data. "Can this user view
--   this specific instance?"</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Secure by Default (Whitelist Approach)</h2>
--   
--   NeoHaskell uses a whitelist approach to authorization:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>**Default to authenticated**: Use <a>authenticatedAccess</a>
--   unless you have a specific reason not to</li>
--   <li>**Public access is opt-in**: Only use <a>publicAccess</a> for
--   truly public data (product catalogs, etc.)</li>
--   <li>**Explicit is better**: Every query must define its auth policy -
--   there are no implicit defaults</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h2>Example Usage</h2>
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- User's private data (RECOMMENDED PATTERN)
--   canAccess = authenticatedAccess
--   canView = ownerOnly (.ownerId)
--   
--   -- Admin-only query
--   canAccess = requirePermission "admin:read"
--   canView = publicView  -- OK: canAccess already restricts to admins
--   
--   -- Truly public data (use sparingly)
--   canAccess = publicAccess  -- EXPLICIT opt-in to public access
--   canView = publicView
--   </pre>

module Auth.Jwt

-- | Validate a JWT token against the auth configuration. Returns
--   UserClaims on success, AuthError on failure.
--   
--   Validation order (per RFC 8725 and performance optimization): 1. Parse
--   JWT header ONCE (optimized - single parse for alg<i>kid</i>typ/crit)
--   2. Reject alg not in allowlist (fail fast, no crypto) 3. Reject
--   unknown crit headers 4. Validate typ if present (RFC 8725) 5. Decode
--   and verify with jose library (CPU-heavy) 6. Issuer validation (exact
--   match) 7. Audience validation 8. Time claims (exp/nbf) with clock skew
--   9. Extract UserClaims
validateToken :: AuthConfig -> Array JWK -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Validate a JWT token using a pre-built JWKSet. This is the optimized
--   hot path - avoids JWKSet construction per request.
validateTokenWithJwkSet :: AuthConfig -> JWKSet -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Validate a JWT token with a pre-parsed header. This is the most
--   optimized hot path for the middleware: 1. Parse header once in
--   middleware (for kid extraction) 2. Pass ParsedHeader here to avoid
--   re-parsing
validateTokenWithParsedHeader :: AuthConfig -> JWKSet -> ParsedHeader -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Extract kid (key ID) from JWT token header. Returns Nothing if kid is
--   not present in the header. Note: For hot path, prefer parseHeader once
--   and access .kid
extractKidFromToken :: Text -> Maybe Text

-- | Parsed JWT header for hot-path optimization. Parse once, use for all
--   validation checks.
data ParsedHeader
ParsedHeader :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> ParsedHeader

-- | Algorithm (required)
[alg] :: ParsedHeader -> Text

-- | Key ID (optional)
[kid] :: ParsedHeader -> Maybe Text

-- | Type header (optional, RFC 8725 recommends checking)
[typ] :: ParsedHeader -> Maybe Text

-- | Critical headers (empty if not present)
[crit] :: ParsedHeader -> Array Text

-- | Parse JWT header once for all validation checks. This is the optimized
--   hot path - avoids re-parsing header 3-4 times. Uses strict base64url
--   decoding per RFC 8725 security recommendations.
parseHeader :: Text -> Result AuthError ParsedHeader

-- | Parse just the token header (for testing/debugging)
parseTokenHeader :: Text -> Result AuthError Text

-- | Extract algorithm from JWT token without full parsing. Note: For hot
--   path, prefer parseHeader once and access .alg
extractAlgorithmFromToken :: Text -> Result AuthError Text
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.Jwt.ParsedHeader
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Jwt.ParsedHeader


-- | Test utilities for JWT validation. Provides functions to generate test
--   tokens for various scenarios.

module Test.Auth.Jwt.Core

-- | Default test configuration
testConfig :: AuthConfig

-- | Test configuration with audience validation
testConfigWithAudience :: Text -> AuthConfig

-- | Pre-computed test keys for signing tokens. Keys are parsed from JSON
--   rather than generated at runtime, eliminating expensive RSA key
--   generation (~300ms per call). These are TEST-ONLY keys (1024-bit RSA)
--   — NOT suitable for production.
testKeys :: Task Text TestKeys

-- | Test keys for signing tokens
data TestKeys
TestKeys :: JWK -> JWK -> JWK -> TestKeys
[es256Key] :: TestKeys -> JWK
[rs256Key] :: TestKeys -> JWK
[hs256Key] :: TestKeys -> JWK

-- | Sign a valid token with ES256
signValidToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a valid token with RS256
signValidTokenRS256 :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign an expired token
signExpiredToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token not yet valid (nbf in future)
signNotYetValidToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token that expired recently (within clock skew tolerance)
signRecentlyExpiredToken :: TestKeys -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with specific issuer
signTokenWithIssuer :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with specific audience
signTokenWithAudience :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with specific sub claim
signTokenWithSub :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with email claim
signTokenWithEmail :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with permissions claim
signTokenWithPermissions :: TestKeys -> Array Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with tenant ID
signTokenWithTenantId :: TestKeys -> Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Sign a token with arbitrary custom claims
signTokenWithClaims :: TestKeys -> Text -> Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Task Text Text

-- | Create a token with alg=none (MUST be rejected) Constructed
--   programmatically at runtime to avoid triggering secret scanners.
--   Header: {"alg":"none","typ":<a>JWT</a>} Payload:
--   {"sub":"user","iss":"<a>https://auth.example.com"}</a>
makeAlgNoneToken :: Text

-- | Create a token signed with HS256 (symmetric, should be rejected for
--   public key validation) Constructed programmatically at runtime to
--   avoid triggering secret scanners. Header:
--   {"alg":<a>HS256</a>,"typ":<a>JWT</a>} Payload:
--   {"sub":"user","iss":"<a>https://auth.example.com"}</a> Note: Signature
--   is a placeholder since we only need to test algorithm rejection.
makeHS256Token :: Text

-- | Create a malformed token for testing format validation
makeMalformedToken :: Text

-- | Validate a JWT token format against the auth configuration. Wrapper
--   that uses empty key set - only validates token format, NOT signatures.
--   For signature validation, use validateTokenWithKeys instead.
validateTokenFormat :: AuthConfig -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Validate a JWT token with a specific key set
validateTokenWithKeys :: AuthConfig -> Array JWK -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError UserClaims)

-- | Create a test JwksManager with pre-populated keys. This manager has no
--   background refresh - it's purely for testing. Keys are extracted from
--   TestKeys and stored directly in the snapshot.
createTestManager :: TestKeys -> Task Text JwksManager


-- | HTTP middleware for JWT authentication. Integrates with WebTransport
--   to authenticate requests.

module Auth.Middleware

-- | Extract Bearer token from Authorization header. Returns Nothing if no
--   Authorization header or not Bearer scheme. Handles whitespace
--   variations: " Bearer token " is accepted.
extractToken :: Request -> Maybe Text

-- | Extract token from ?token= query parameter. SECURITY: Only use for
--   endpoints where Authorization header is infeasible (e.g., browser
--   redirect flows like <i>connect</i>{provider}). Prefer
--   <a>extractToken</a> (header-based) for all other endpoints. See
--   ADR-0031.
extractTokenFromQuery :: Request -> Maybe Text

-- | Check authentication and authorization for a request. Returns Ok
--   AuthContext on success, Err AuthError on failure.
checkAuth :: Maybe JwksManager -> AuthConfig -> AuthOptions -> Request -> Task err (Result AuthError AuthContext)

-- | Validate a pre-extracted token and build auth context. SECURITY: Use
--   when the token is obtained from a non-standard source (e.g., query
--   parameter for browser redirect flows). The token receives the same
--   validation as header-extracted tokens. See ADR-0031.
checkAuthWithToken :: Maybe JwksManager -> AuthConfig -> Text -> Task err (Result AuthError AuthContext)

-- | Convert AuthError to appropriate HTTP response. SECURITY: Uses generic
--   messages to prevent information leakage.
respondWithAuthError :: AuthError -> (Response -> Task Text ResponseReceived) -> Task Text ResponseReceived

-- | Authentication context passed to handlers.
data AuthContext
AuthContext :: Maybe UserClaims -> Bool -> AuthContext

-- | User claims (Nothing if endpoint is public)
[claims] :: AuthContext -> Maybe UserClaims

-- | Whether the request was authenticated
[isAuthenticated] :: AuthContext -> Bool
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Auth.Middleware.AuthContext
instance GHC.Show.Show Auth.Middleware.AuthContext


-- | JWT Authentication for NeoHaskell.
--   
--   This module provides JWT token validation for event-sourced
--   applications. It's designed to work with external OAuth providers
--   (Keycloak, Auth0, Okta, etc.) using OpenID Connect Discovery.
--   
--   <h1>Quick Start</h1>
--   
--   <pre>
--   app :: Application
--   app =
--     Application.new
--       |&gt; Application.withAuth "<a>https://auth.example.com"</a>
--       |&gt; Application.withService myService
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>What Jess Sees</h1>
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><a>UserClaims</a> - The authenticated user's identity</li>
--   <li><a>AuthOptions</a> - Declarative auth requirements (Everyone,
--   Authenticated, etc.)</li>
--   <li><a>AuthOverrides</a> - Optional advanced configuration</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   Everything else (JWKS caching, token validation, etc.) is internal.

module Auth

-- | User identity extracted from a validated JWT. Available to command
--   handlers when authentication is required.
data UserClaims
UserClaims :: Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Array Text -> Maybe Text -> Map Text Value -> UserClaims

-- | Subject (user ID from OAuth provider)
[sub] :: UserClaims -> Text

-- | User email (if provided)
[email] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Display name (if provided)
[name] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | Scopes<i>permissions</i>roles
[permissions] :: UserClaims -> Array Text

-- | For multi-tenant apps
[tenantId] :: UserClaims -> Maybe Text

-- | All claims for custom access
[rawClaims] :: UserClaims -> Map Text Value

-- | Authentication requirements for an endpoint. Note: Permission checks
--   belong in the command's decide method.
data AuthOptions

-- | No authentication required (public endpoint)
Everyone :: AuthOptions

-- | Valid JWT required
Authenticated :: AuthOptions

-- | Optional overrides for advanced users. Most fields use sensible
--   defaults; only override if you have special requirements.
data AuthOverrides
AuthOverrides :: Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Text -> Maybe Int64 -> Maybe (Array Text) -> Maybe (Array Text) -> AuthOverrides

-- | Override aud claim validation (default: None)
[audience] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Text

-- | Override permissions claim name (default: "permissions")
[permissionsClaim] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Text

-- | Claim name for tenant ID (default: None)
[tenantIdClaim] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Text

-- | Override clock skew tolerance (default: 60)
[clockSkewSeconds] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe Int64

-- | Override allowed algorithms
[allowedAlgorithms] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe (Array Text)

-- | Restrict IdP to specific domains (e.g., ["auth.example.com",
--   "login.company.eu"]) When set, discovery will reject URLs from
--   non-allowlisted domains. Default: None (allow any HTTPS URL that
--   passes SSRF checks)
[allowedIdpDomains] :: AuthOverrides -> Maybe (Array Text)

-- | Default overrides (all Nothing - use discovered/default values).
defaultOverrides :: AuthOverrides

-- | Errors that can occur during authentication. These are mapped to HTTP
--   responses by the middleware. Note: Error responses use generic
--   messages to prevent information leakage.
data AuthError

-- | No Authorization header
TokenMissing :: AuthError

-- | Not a valid JWT format
TokenMalformed :: Text -> AuthError

-- | exp claim in the past
TokenExpired :: AuthError

-- | nbf claim in the future
TokenNotYetValid :: AuthError

-- | Signature doesn't verify
SignatureInvalid :: AuthError

-- | alg not in allowlist (RFC 8725)
AlgorithmNotAllowed :: Text -> AuthError

-- | Unknown crit header (RFC 8725)
UnsupportedCritHeader :: Array Text -> AuthError

-- | Key type doesn't match alg (RFC 8725)
KeyAlgorithmMismatch :: AuthError

-- | Expected vs actual issuer
IssuerMismatch :: Text -> Text -> AuthError

-- | Expected vs actual audience
AudienceMismatch :: Text -> Array Text -> AuthError

-- | JWKS/keys unavailable (503, not 401)
AuthInfraUnavailable :: Text -> AuthError

-- | Key ID not found in JWKS
KeyNotFound :: Text -> AuthError


module Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core

-- | A simple test query type for testing QueryObjectStore.
data TestQuery
TestQuery :: Uuid -> Text -> Int -> TestQuery
[queryId] :: TestQuery -> Uuid
[name] :: TestQuery -> Text
[count] :: TestQuery -> Int
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance GHC.Show.Show Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.Core.TestQuery


module Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.Context
data Context
Context :: QueryObjectStore TestQuery -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Context
[store] :: Context -> QueryObjectStore TestQuery
[testId1] :: Context -> Uuid
[testId2] :: Context -> Uuid
[testId3] :: Context -> Uuid
initialize :: Task Text (QueryObjectStore TestQuery) -> Task Text Context


module Test.Service.EventStore.Core

-- | Example domain events for a shopping cart Note: The Event instance is
--   defined in Test.Service.Command.Core where EntityOf CartEvent =
--   CartEntity is also defined
data CartEvent
CartCreated :: Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
ItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: CartEvent -> Int
ItemRemoved :: Uuid -> Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
CartCheckedOut :: Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
newInsertion :: Int -> Task w (Insertion CartEvent)
instance Data.Default.Internal.Default Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance GHC.Show.Show Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent

module Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core

-- | Example domain events for a shopping cart Note: The Event instance is
--   defined in Test.Service.Command.Core where EntityOf CartEvent =
--   CartEntity is also defined
data CartEvent
CartCreated :: Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
ItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: CartEvent -> Int
ItemRemoved :: Uuid -> Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid
CartCheckedOut :: Uuid -> CartEvent
[entityId] :: CartEvent -> Uuid

-- | Example entity state for a shopping cart
data CartState
CartState :: Maybe Uuid -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> Int -> CartState
[cartId] :: CartState -> Maybe Uuid
[cartItems] :: CartState -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[isCheckedOut] :: CartState -> Bool
[version] :: CartState -> Int

-- | Initial state for a new cart
initialState :: CartState

-- | Apply a cart event to the current state
applyEvent :: CartEvent -> CartState -> CartState

-- | Create a new entity fetcher for carts
newFetcher :: EventStore CartEvent -> Task Error (EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent)

-- | Create a new entity fetcher for carts with cache support
newFetcherWithCache :: EventStore CartEvent -> SnapshotCache CartState -> Task Error (EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance GHC.Show.Show Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState
instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Core.CartState


module Test.Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.Context
data Context
Context :: SnapshotCache CartState -> StreamId -> EntityName -> Context
[cache] :: Context -> SnapshotCache CartState
[streamId] :: Context -> StreamId
[entityName] :: Context -> EntityName
initialize :: Task Text (SnapshotCache CartState) -> Task Text Context


module Test.Service.EntityFetcher.FetchWithCache.Context
data Context
Context :: EventStore CartEvent -> EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent -> SnapshotCache CartState -> StreamId -> EntityName -> Context
[store] :: Context -> EventStore CartEvent
[fetcher] :: Context -> EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent
[cache] :: Context -> SnapshotCache CartState
[streamId] :: Context -> StreamId
[entityName] :: Context -> EntityName
initialize :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent, SnapshotCache CartState) -> Task Text Context


module Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Fetch.Context
data Context
Context :: EventStore CartEvent -> EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent -> StreamId -> EntityName -> Context
[store] :: Context -> EventStore CartEvent
[fetcher] :: Context -> EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent
[streamId] :: Context -> StreamId
[entityName] :: Context -> EntityName
initialize :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartState CartEvent) -> Task Text Context

module Test.Service.Command.Core
data AddItemToCart
AddItemToCart :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> AddItemToCart
[cartId] :: AddItemToCart -> Uuid
[itemId] :: AddItemToCart -> Uuid
[amount] :: AddItemToCart -> Int
data RemoveItemFromCart
RemoveItemFromCart :: Uuid -> Uuid -> RemoveItemFromCart
[cartId] :: RemoveItemFromCart -> Uuid
[itemId] :: RemoveItemFromCart -> Uuid
data CheckoutCart
CheckoutCart :: Uuid -> CheckoutCart
[cartId] :: CheckoutCart -> Uuid

-- | Command that requires authentication. Rejects if ctx.user is Nothing.
data AuthenticatedAddItem
AuthenticatedAddItem :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> AuthenticatedAddItem
[cartId] :: AuthenticatedAddItem -> Uuid
[itemId] :: AuthenticatedAddItem -> Uuid
[amount] :: AuthenticatedAddItem -> Int

-- | Command that checks ownership. Rejects if ctx.user.sub doesn't match
--   entity.ownerId.
data OwnedCartCheckout
OwnedCartCheckout :: Uuid -> OwnedCartCheckout
[cartId] :: OwnedCartCheckout -> Uuid
data CartEntity
CartEntity :: Uuid -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> CartEntity
[cartId] :: CartEntity -> Uuid
[cartItems] :: CartEntity -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[cartCheckedOut] :: CartEntity -> Bool

-- | Cart entity with owner tracking for authorization tests.
data OwnedCartEntity
OwnedCartEntity :: Uuid -> Text -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> OwnedCartEntity
[cartId] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Uuid
[ownerId] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Text
[cartItems] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[cartCheckedOut] :: OwnedCartEntity -> Bool
initialCartState :: CartEntity
initialOwnedCartState :: OwnedCartEntity
applyCartEvent :: CartEvent -> CartEntity -> CartEntity
applyOwnedCartEvent :: OwnedCartEvent -> OwnedCartEntity -> OwnedCartEntity

-- | Events for owned cart.
data OwnedCartEvent
OwnedCartCreated :: Uuid -> Text -> OwnedCartEvent
[entityId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
[ownerId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Text
OwnedItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> OwnedCartEvent
[entityId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Int
OwnedCartCheckedOut :: Uuid -> OwnedCartEvent
[entityId] :: OwnedCartEvent -> Uuid
data TenantAddItem
TenantAddItem :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> TenantAddItem
[cartId] :: TenantAddItem -> Uuid
[itemId] :: TenantAddItem -> Uuid
[amount] :: TenantAddItem -> Int
data TenantCreateCart
TenantCreateCart :: TenantCreateCart
data TenantCartEntity
TenantCartEntity :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Array (Uuid, Int) -> Bool -> TenantCartEntity
[cartId] :: TenantCartEntity -> Uuid
[tenantId] :: TenantCartEntity -> Uuid
[cartItems] :: TenantCartEntity -> Array (Uuid, Int)
[cartCheckedOut] :: TenantCartEntity -> Bool
data TenantCartEvent
TenantCartCreated :: Uuid -> Uuid -> TenantCartEvent
[entityId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
[tenantId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
TenantItemAdded :: Uuid -> Uuid -> Int -> TenantCartEvent
[entityId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
[itemId] :: TenantCartEvent -> Uuid
[amount] :: TenantCartEvent -> Int
initialTenantCartState :: TenantCartEntity
applyTenantCartEvent :: TenantCartEvent -> TenantCartEntity -> TenantCartEntity
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.AddItemToCart
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.AuthenticatedAddItem
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.CheckoutCart
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartCheckout
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.RemoveItemFromCart
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantAddItem
instance Service.Command.Core.Command Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCreateCart
instance Service.Entity.Core.Entity Test.Service.Command.Core.CartEntity
instance Service.Entity.Core.Entity Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEntity
instance Service.Entity.Core.Entity Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEntity
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.AddItemToCart
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.AuthenticatedAddItem
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.CartEntity
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.CheckoutCart
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartCheckout
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEntity
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEvent
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.RemoveItemFromCart
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantAddItem
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEntity
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEvent
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCreateCart
instance Service.Entity.Core.Event Test.Service.EventStore.Core.CartEvent
instance Service.Entity.Core.Event Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEvent
instance Service.Entity.Core.Event Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEvent

instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.CartEntity
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.CheckoutCart
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartCheckout
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEntity
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEvent
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.RemoveItemFromCart
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantAddItem
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEntity
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEvent
instance Data.Aeson.Types.FromJSON.FromJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCreateCart

instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEvent

instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEntity
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCartEvent
instance GHC.Generics.Generic Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCreateCart
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.AddItemToCart
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.AuthenticatedAddItem
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.CartEntity
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.CheckoutCart
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartCheckout
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.OwnedCartEntity
instance GHC.Classes.Ord Test.Service.Command.Core.RemoveItemFromCart

instance Data.Aeson.Types.ToJSON.ToJSON Test.Service.Command.Core.TenantCreateCart


module Test.Service.CommandHandler.Execute.Context
data Context
Context :: EventStore CartEvent -> EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent -> StreamId -> EntityName -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Uuid -> Context
[cartStore] :: Context -> EventStore CartEvent
[cartFetcher] :: Context -> EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent
[cartStreamId] :: Context -> StreamId
[cartEntityName] :: Context -> EntityName
[cartId] :: Context -> Uuid
[itemId1] :: Context -> Uuid
[itemId2] :: Context -> Uuid
initialize :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Task Text Context


module Test.Spec
type Spec a = SpecWith a

-- | Describe a group of tests
describe :: Text -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Define a test case
it :: HasCallStack => Text -> (context -> Task Text Unit) -> Spec context

-- | Define a focused test case (runs only this test)
only_it :: HasCallStack => Text -> (context -> Task Text Unit) -> Spec context

-- | Assert that two values are equal
shouldBe :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that two values are not equal
shouldNotBe :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value satisfies a predicate
shouldSatisfy :: (HasCallStack, Show a) => (a -> Bool) -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that an array contains another array
shouldContain :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => Array a -> Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that an array does not contain another array
shouldNotContain :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => Array a -> Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a text starts with a prefix
shouldStartWith :: HasCallStack => Text -> Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a string ends with a suffix
shouldEndWith :: HasCallStack => Text -> Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that two lists contain the same elements, regardless of order
shouldMatchList :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a) => Array a -> Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a task returns a specific value
shouldReturn :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Eq a, Show err) => a -> Task err a -> Task err2 Unit

-- | Assert that an array is in increasing order
shouldHaveIncreasingOrder :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Marks a test as pending
pending :: Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Mark a group of tests as pending
xdescribe :: Text -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Run tests in parallel
parallel :: Spec -> Spec

-- | Run tests sequentially
sequential :: Spec -> Spec

-- | Run a Task in a test
runTask :: (HasCallStack, Show err) => Task err a -> IO a

-- | Fail the test with a message
fail :: HasCallStack => Text -> Task Text Unit

-- | Run a Task before the tests
beforeAll :: (HasCallStack, Show err, AutoCleanup context) => Task err context -> Spec context -> Spec Unit

-- | Run a Task before each test
before :: (HasCallStack, Show err, AutoCleanup context) => Task err context -> Spec context -> Spec Unit

-- | Run a Task after each test
after :: (HasCallStack, Show err) => Task err Unit -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Run a Task once after all tests in a group
afterAll :: (HasCallStack, Show err) => Task err Unit -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Runs a block of tests if an environment variable is set
whenEnvVar :: HasCallStack => Text -> Spec Unit -> Spec Unit

-- | Assert that an array is in decreasing order
shouldHaveDecreasingOrder :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => Array a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is less than or equal to a maximum value
shouldBeLessThanOrEqual :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is greater than or equal to a minimum value
shouldBeGreaterThanOrEqual :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is less than a maximum value
shouldBeLessThan :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that a value is greater than a minimum value
shouldBeGreaterThan :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => a -> a -> Task Text Unit

-- | Modifier that allows asserting the contents of a Var
varContents :: (HasCallStack, Show a, Ord a) => (a -> a -> Task Text Unit) -> a -> Var a -> Task Text Unit
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.Command.Decide.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.IndividualStreamOrdering.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.ReadAllBackwardsFromEnd.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.ReadAllForwardsFromStart.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.CommandHandler.Execute.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EntityFetcher.FetchWithCache.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EntityFetcher.Fetch.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.BatchValidation.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.Subscriptions.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.StreamTruncation.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.OptimisticConcurrency.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup Test.Service.EventStore.GlobalStreamOrdering.Context.Context
instance Test.Spec.AutoCleanup (Service.EventStore.Core.EventStore eventType)


module Test.Service.CommandHandler.Core


module Test.CompileTime

-- | Assert that an expression should not typecheck. This is useful for
--   testing type-level constraints and validation. Note: Requires {-#
--   OPTIONS_GHC -fdefer-type-errors #-} in test files.
--   
--   Example: <tt> it "should not allow adding Int to String" _ -&gt; do
--   shouldNotTypecheck (5 + "hello") </tt>
--   
--   Example with type families: <tt> it "should reject invalid type family
--   application" _ -&gt; do shouldNotTypecheck (undefined :: MyTypeFamily
--   Int String) </tt>
shouldNotTypecheck :: (HasCallStack, NFData value) => (() ~ () => value) -> Task Text Unit

-- | Assert that an expression should typecheck. This is the opposite of
--   shouldNotTypecheck - it verifies that valid code compiles. Note: This
--   is mainly for documentation - if code typechecks, it compiles
--   normally.
--   
--   Example: <tt> it "should allow adding two Ints" _ -&gt; do
--   shouldTypecheck (5 + 3) </tt>
--   
--   Example with type families: <tt> it "should accept valid type family
--   application" _ -&gt; do shouldTypecheck (undefined :: MyTypeFamily Int
--   Int) </tt>
shouldTypecheck :: NFData value => value -> Task Text Unit

-- | A class of types that can be fully evaluated.
class NFData a

-- | <a>rnf</a> should reduce its argument to normal form (that is, fully
--   evaluate all sub-components), and then return <tt>()</tt>.
--   
--   <h3><a>Generic</a> <a>NFData</a> deriving</h3>
--   
--   Starting with GHC 7.2, you can automatically derive instances for
--   types possessing a <a>Generic</a> instance.
--   
--   Note: <a>Generic1</a> can be auto-derived starting with GHC 7.4
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric #-}
--   
--   import GHC.Generics (Generic, Generic1)
--   import Control.DeepSeq
--   
--   data Foo a = Foo a String
--                deriving (Eq, Generic, Generic1)
--   
--   instance NFData a =&gt; NFData (Foo a)
--   instance NFData1 Foo
--   
--   data Colour = Red | Green | Blue
--                 deriving Generic
--   
--   instance NFData Colour
--   </pre>
--   
--   Starting with GHC 7.10, the example above can be written more
--   concisely by enabling the new <tt>DeriveAnyClass</tt> extension:
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric, DeriveAnyClass #-}
--   
--   import GHC.Generics (Generic)
--   import Control.DeepSeq
--   
--   data Foo a = Foo a String
--                deriving (Eq, Generic, Generic1, NFData, NFData1)
--   
--   data Colour = Red | Green | Blue
--                 deriving (Generic, NFData)
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h3>Compatibility with previous <tt>deepseq</tt> versions</h3>
--   
--   Prior to version 1.4.0.0, the default implementation of the <a>rnf</a>
--   method was defined as
--   
--   <pre>
--   <a>rnf</a> a = <a>seq</a> a ()
--   </pre>
--   
--   However, starting with <tt>deepseq-1.4.0.0</tt>, the default
--   implementation is based on <tt>DefaultSignatures</tt> allowing for
--   more accurate auto-derived <a>NFData</a> instances. If you need the
--   previously used exact default <a>rnf</a> method implementation
--   semantics, use
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance NFData Colour where rnf x = seq x ()
--   </pre>
--   
--   or alternatively
--   
--   <pre>
--   instance NFData Colour where rnf = rwhnf
--   </pre>
--   
--   or
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE BangPatterns #-}
--   instance NFData Colour where rnf !_ = ()
--   </pre>
rnf :: NFData a => a -> ()
($dmrnf) :: (NFData a, Generic a, GNFData Zero (Rep a)) => a -> ()


module Test.AppSpec.Scenario
data Scenario appModel
Scenario :: Text -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit -> Scenario appModel
[name] :: Scenario appModel -> Text
[steps] :: Scenario appModel -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
data ScenarioSteps appModel result
ScenarioSteps :: ScenarioSteps appModel result
empty :: ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
instance GHC.Base.Applicative (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.Scenario appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Base.Functor (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Base.Monad (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.Scenario appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.Scenario appModel)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.Scenario.ScenarioSteps appModel result)


module Test.AppSpec.AppSpec
data AppSpec appModel
AppSpec :: Array (Scenario appModel) -> AppSpec appModel
[scenarios] :: AppSpec appModel -> Array (Scenario appModel)
data AppSpecSteps appModel result
AppSpecSteps :: Array (Scenario appModel) -> result -> AppSpecSteps appModel result
[stepScenarios] :: AppSpecSteps appModel result -> Array (Scenario appModel)
[result] :: AppSpecSteps appModel result -> result
fromSteps :: AppSpecSteps appModel Unit -> AppSpec appModel
emptySteps :: AppSpecSteps appModel Unit
instance GHC.Base.Applicative (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpec appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq result => GHC.Classes.Eq (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Base.Functor (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Base.Monad (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpec appModel)
instance GHC.Classes.Ord result => GHC.Classes.Ord (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel result)
instance GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpec appModel)
instance GHC.Show.Show result => GHC.Show.Show (Test.AppSpec.AppSpec.AppSpecSteps appModel result)


module Test.AppSpec.Verify
run :: Ops appModel -> AppSpec appModel -> Task Text Unit
data Ops appModel
Ops :: (Scenario appModel -> Task Text Unit) -> Ops appModel
[verifyScenario] :: Ops appModel -> Scenario appModel -> Task Text Unit
defaultOps :: Ops appModel


module Test.AppSpec
data AppSpec appModel
specificationFor :: appModel -> AppSpecSteps appModel Unit -> AppSpec appModel
data Scenario appModel
scenario :: Text -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit -> AppSpecSteps appModel Unit
given :: commandPattern -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
expect :: outcomePattern -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
and :: outcomePattern -> ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
receivedCommand :: adapter
registeredEvent :: adapter
executedTask :: adapter
verifyAppSpec :: AppSpec appModel -> Task Text Unit
emptyScenario :: ScenarioSteps appModel Unit
noScenarios :: AppSpecSteps appModel Unit


module Test


module Test.Service.SnapshotCache.InMemory.Spec
spec :: Task Text (SnapshotCache CartState) -> Spec Unit


module Test.Service.SnapshotCache
spec :: Task Text (SnapshotCache CartState) -> Spec Unit


module Test.Service.QueryObjectStore.InMemory.Spec
spec :: Task Text (QueryObjectStore TestQuery) -> Spec Unit


module Test.Service.QueryObjectStore
spec :: Task Text (QueryObjectStore TestQuery) -> Spec Unit


-- | Shared specification tests for FileStateStore implementations.
--   
--   Both InMemory and PostgreSQL FileStateStore implementations should
--   pass these tests.
--   
--   Usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   -- In InMemorySpec.hs
--   import Test.Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore qualified as FileStateStoreSpec
--   
--   spec = do
--     describe <a>InMemoryFileStateStore</a> do
--       let newStore = do
--             stateMap &lt;- FileUpload.newInMemoryFileStateStore
--             Task.yield (FileUpload.inMemoryFileStateStore stateMap)
--       FileStateStoreSpec.spec newStore
--   </pre>

module Test.Service.FileUpload.FileStateStore
spec :: Task Text FileStateStore -> Spec Unit


module Test.Service.EventStore.Subscriptions.Spec
spec :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent) -> Spec Unit

module Test.Service.EventStore.ReadAllBackwardsFromEnd.Spec

module Test.Service.EventStore.OptimisticConcurrency.Spec

module Test.Service.EventStore.LocalPositionStamping.Spec

module Test.Service.EventStore

fetchResultToMaybe :: EntityFetchResult state -> Maybe state

spec :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Spec Unit
basicExecutionSpecs :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Spec Unit
retryLogicSpecs :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Spec Unit
concurrencySpecs :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Spec Unit
errorScenarioSpecs :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent, EntityFetcher CartEntity CartEvent) -> Spec Unit


module Test.Service.CommandHandler
spec :: Task Text (EventStore CartEvent) -> Spec Unit


module Test.Service.Command.Decide.Spec
runTestDecision :: HasCallStack => Decision event -> Task Text (CommandResult event)
spec :: Spec Unit
cartCommandSpecs :: Spec Unit
edgeCaseSpecs :: Spec Unit

-- | Helper to create a test user with given sub (user ID)
testUser :: Text -> UserClaims

-- | Helper to create an authenticated RequestContext
authenticatedContext :: Text -> RequestContext
authorizationSpecs :: Spec Unit


module Test.Service.Command
spec :: Spec Unit


-- | Shared TH primitives for concept-marker boilerplate emission.
--   
--   Internal to nhcore. Used by Service.CommandExecutor.TH,
--   Service.Query.TH, and Service.Event.TH to emit the JSON and
--   <tt>deriving stock</tt> instances every command, event, and query type
--   needs, while remaining a no-op when an instance is already in scope
--   (so consumers can declare custom encodings before the marker call).

module Config.Core

-- | A single field definition within a config type.
--   
--   Built incrementally using the Config.Builder combinators:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--   </pre>
data FieldDef
FieldDef :: Text -> Type -> [FieldModifier] -> FieldDef

-- | Field name (becomes the record field name)
[fieldName] :: FieldDef -> Text

-- | The Haskell type of this field
[fieldType] :: FieldDef -> Type

-- | Accumulated modifiers
[fieldModifiers] :: FieldDef -> [FieldModifier]

-- | Modifiers that can be applied to a config field.
--   
--   These are accumulated by builder functions and converted to
--   opt-env-conf builders during TH expansion.
data FieldModifier

-- | Documentation string (shown in --help and error messages)
ModDoc :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Environment variable name override
ModEnvVar :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Default value (as a TH Q Exp for splicing)
ModDefault :: Q Exp -> FieldModifier

-- | Field is required (mutually exclusive with ModDefault)
ModRequired :: FieldModifier

-- | Sensitive value - redacted in logs and --help
ModSecret :: FieldModifier

-- | Long CLI option name (e.g., "port" for --port)
ModCliLong :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Short CLI option character (e.g., <tt>p</tt> for -p)
ModCliShort :: Char -> FieldModifier

-- | Environment variable prefix for nested configs
ModEnvPrefix :: Text -> FieldModifier

-- | Mark field as a nested config (uses subSettings instead of setting)
ModNested :: FieldModifier

-- | Validation errors detected at compile time.
data ConfigError

-- | Field is missing Config.doc
MissingDoc :: Text -> ConfigError

-- | Field has neither defaultsTo nor required
MissingDefaultOrRequired :: Text -> ConfigError

-- | Field has both defaultsTo and required (invalid)
BothDefaultAndRequired :: Text -> ConfigError

-- | Validate a field definition, returning any errors found.
validateFieldDef :: FieldDef -> [ConfigError]
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Config.Core.ConfigError
instance GHC.Show.Show Config.Core.ConfigError


-- | Template Haskell macro for the NeoHaskell Config DSL.
--   
--   This module provides the <a>defineConfig</a> macro that generates:
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>A data type with the specified fields</li>
--   <li>A <tt>HasParser</tt> instance for opt-env-conf integration</li>
--   <li>A <tt>HasXxxConfig</tt> type alias for implicit parameter
--   access</li>
--   </ol>

module Config.TH

-- | Define a configuration type with the given name and fields.
--   
--   This macro generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>A data type: <tt>data AppConfig = AppConfig { field1 :: Type1, ...
--   }</tt></li>
--   <li>A custom Show instance that redacts secret fields</li>
--   <li>A HasParser instance using opt-env-conf</li>
--   <li>A type alias: <tt>type HasAppConfig = (?config ::
--   AppConfig)</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   All fields must have <a>doc</a> and either <a>defaultsTo</a> or
--   <a>required</a>.
defineConfig :: Text -> [FieldDef] -> Q [Dec]

-- | Convert camelCase field name to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE env var name.
--   
--   Examples: * <tt>port</tt> -&gt; <tt>PORT</tt> * <tt>databaseUrl</tt>
--   -&gt; <tt>DATABASE_URL</tt> * <tt>openRouterKey</tt> -&gt;
--   <tt>OPEN_ROUTER_KEY</tt> * <tt>oauth2ClientId</tt> -&gt;
--   <tt>OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID</tt> * <tt>http2Enabled</tt> -&gt;
--   <tt>HTTP2_ENABLED</tt>
toEnvVarName :: Text -> Text


-- | Builder combinators for the Config DSL.
--   
--   These functions provide a fluent API for defining configuration
--   fields:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--     |&gt; Config.envVar <a>PORT</a>
--     |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>
--   </pre>

module Config.Builder

-- | Start defining a configuration field with the given type and name.
--   
--   The type is captured via TypeApplications:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--   Config.field @(Maybe Int) "optionalPort"
--   </pre>
field :: forall {k} (fieldType :: k). Typeable fieldType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define an enum configuration field.
--   
--   This is semantically equivalent to <a>field</a> but makes the intent
--   clear when defining enum-typed configuration. The enum type must have
--   <tt>Read</tt> and <a>Show</a> instances for parsing from strings.
--   
--   <pre>
--   data LogLevel = Debug | Info | Warn | Error
--     deriving (Show, Read, Generic)
--   
--   Config.enum @LogLevel "logLevel"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "Minimum log level"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo Info
--   </pre>
enum :: forall {k} (enumType :: k). Typeable enumType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define a nested configuration field.
--   
--   Nested configs allow composing multiple config types together. The
--   nested type must also have a <tt>HasParser</tt> instance (typically
--   from its own <tt>defineConfig</tt> declaration).
--   
--   Use <a>envPrefix</a> to namespace the nested config's environment
--   variables.
--   
--   <pre>
--   defineConfig <a>DatabaseConfig</a>
--     [ Config.field @Text "host"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database host"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo "localhost"
--     , Config.field @Int "port"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database port"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 5432
--     ]
--   
--   defineConfig <a>AppConfig</a>
--     [ Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database connection settings"
--         |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   With <tt>envPrefix <a>DB_</a></tt>, the nested fields become:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>DB_HOST</tt> (defaults to "localhost")</li>
--   <li><tt>DB_PORT</tt> (defaults to 5432)</li>
--   </ul>
nested :: forall {k} (nestedType :: k). Typeable nestedType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Add documentation for a field.
--   
--   Documentation is REQUIRED and will cause a compile-time error if
--   missing. It appears in <tt>--help</tt> output and error messages.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--   </pre>
doc :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Specify the environment variable name for a field.
--   
--   If not specified, the field name is converted to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
--   <tt>databaseUrl</tt> becomes <tt>DATABASE_URL</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.envVar <a>DATABASE_URL</a>
--   </pre>
envVar :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set a default value for a field.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>required</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   The value must have a Lift instance so it can be spliced into the
--   generated code.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--   </pre>
defaultsTo :: Lift value => value -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as required.
--   
--   Required fields must be provided via CLI, environment variable, or
--   config file. Missing required fields cause startup to fail with a
--   helpful error message.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>defaultsTo</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
required :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as containing sensitive data.
--   
--   Secret fields: - Are redacted in <tt>--help</tt> output - Are redacted
--   in error messages - Should use the <tt>Secret</tt> wrapper type for
--   additional protection
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--     |&gt; Config.secret
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
secret :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a long CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"  -- enables --port 8080
--   </pre>
cliLong :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a short CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>  -- enables -p 8080
--   </pre>
cliShort :: Char -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set environment variable prefix for nested configs.
--   
--   When using nested configs, this prefix is prepended to all nested
--   field environment variable names.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--     |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--   -- Nested fields: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, etc.
--   </pre>
envPrefix :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef


-- | Declarative configuration DSL for NeoHaskell applications.
--   
--   This module provides a type-safe, fail-fast configuration system that
--   validates all configuration at startup before the application accepts
--   requests.
--   
--   <h1>Quick Start</h1>
--   
--   Define your config using <a>defineConfig</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   {-# LANGUAGE TemplateHaskell #-}
--   module MyApp.Config where
--   
--   import Config (defineConfig)
--   import Config qualified
--   
--   defineConfig <a>AppConfig</a>
--     [ Config.field @Int "port"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--         |&gt; Config.envVar <a>PORT</a>
--         |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"
--         |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>
--   
--     , Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "PostgreSQL connection string"
--         |&gt; Config.required
--         |&gt; Config.envVar <a>DATABASE_URL</a>
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   This generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><pre>data AppConfig = AppConfig { port :: Int, databaseUrl :: Text
--   }</pre></li>
--   <li><tt>instance HasParser AppConfig</tt> (for opt-env-conf)</li>
--   <li><tt>type HasAppConfig = (?config :: AppConfig)</tt> (for implicit
--   parameter access)</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   <h1>Usage Patterns</h1>
--   
--   <h2>Via Application Framework (Recommended)</h2>
--   
--   Register config with <a>withConfig</a> for automatic loading and
--   injection:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app :: Application
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withConfig @AppConfig
--     |&gt; Application.withService myService
--   
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = Application.run app |&gt; Task.runOrPanic
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Explicit Access</h2>
--   
--   After <a>run</a>, access config anywhere via <a>get</a>:
--   
--   <pre>
--   myFunction :: Task err ()
--   myFunction = do
--     let port = (Config.get @AppConfig).port
--     ...
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h2>Direct Loading (Advanced)</h2>
--   
--   For standalone scripts or testing, use <a>load</a> directly:
--   
--   <pre>
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     config <a>Config.load @AppConfig |</a> Task.runOrPanic
--     let ?config = config
--     runApp
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Accessing Configuration</h1>
--   
--   With the implicit parameter in scope, access config fields directly:
--   
--   <pre>
--   startServer :: (HasAppConfig) =&gt; Task Error ()
--   startServer = do
--     Console.printLine [fmt|Starting on port {?config.port}|]
--     ...
--   </pre>
--   
--   <h1>Field Modifiers</h1>
--   
--   | Modifier | Description | |----------|-------------| | <a>doc</a> |
--   Documentation (REQUIRED) | | <a>envVar</a> | Environment variable name
--   | | <a>defaultsTo</a> | Default value | | <a>required</a> | Must be
--   provided | | <a>secret</a> | Redacted in output | | <a>cliLong</a> |
--   Long CLI flag | | <a>cliShort</a> | Short CLI flag | |
--   <a>envPrefix</a> | Prefix for nested configs |

module Config

-- | Define a configuration type with the given name and fields.
--   
--   This macro generates:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>A data type: <tt>data AppConfig = AppConfig { field1 :: Type1, ...
--   }</tt></li>
--   <li>A custom Show instance that redacts secret fields</li>
--   <li>A HasParser instance using opt-env-conf</li>
--   <li>A type alias: <tt>type HasAppConfig = (?config ::
--   AppConfig)</tt></li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   All fields must have <a>doc</a> and either <a>defaultsTo</a> or
--   <a>required</a>.
defineConfig :: Text -> [FieldDef] -> Q [Dec]

-- | Start defining a configuration field with the given type and name.
--   
--   The type is captured via TypeApplications:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--   Config.field @(Maybe Int) "optionalPort"
--   </pre>
field :: forall {k} (fieldType :: k). Typeable fieldType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define an enum configuration field.
--   
--   This is semantically equivalent to <a>field</a> but makes the intent
--   clear when defining enum-typed configuration. The enum type must have
--   <tt>Read</tt> and <a>Show</a> instances for parsing from strings.
--   
--   <pre>
--   data LogLevel = Debug | Info | Warn | Error
--     deriving (Show, Read, Generic)
--   
--   Config.enum @LogLevel "logLevel"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "Minimum log level"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo Info
--   </pre>
enum :: forall {k} (enumType :: k). Typeable enumType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Define a nested configuration field.
--   
--   Nested configs allow composing multiple config types together. The
--   nested type must also have a <tt>HasParser</tt> instance (typically
--   from its own <tt>defineConfig</tt> declaration).
--   
--   Use <a>envPrefix</a> to namespace the nested config's environment
--   variables.
--   
--   <pre>
--   defineConfig <a>DatabaseConfig</a>
--     [ Config.field @Text "host"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database host"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo "localhost"
--     , Config.field @Int "port"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database port"
--         |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 5432
--     ]
--   
--   defineConfig <a>AppConfig</a>
--     [ Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--         |&gt; Config.doc "Database connection settings"
--         |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--     ]
--   </pre>
--   
--   With <tt>envPrefix <a>DB_</a></tt>, the nested fields become:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li><tt>DB_HOST</tt> (defaults to "localhost")</li>
--   <li><tt>DB_PORT</tt> (defaults to 5432)</li>
--   </ul>
nested :: forall {k} (nestedType :: k). Typeable nestedType => Text -> FieldDef

-- | Add documentation for a field.
--   
--   Documentation is REQUIRED and will cause a compile-time error if
--   missing. It appears in <tt>--help</tt> output and error messages.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.doc "HTTP port to listen on"
--   </pre>
doc :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Specify the environment variable name for a field.
--   
--   If not specified, the field name is converted to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
--   <tt>databaseUrl</tt> becomes <tt>DATABASE_URL</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.envVar <a>DATABASE_URL</a>
--   </pre>
envVar :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set a default value for a field.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>required</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   The value must have a Lift instance so it can be spliced into the
--   generated code.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.defaultsTo 8080
--   </pre>
defaultsTo :: Lift value => value -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as required.
--   
--   Required fields must be provided via CLI, environment variable, or
--   config file. Missing required fields cause startup to fail with a
--   helpful error message.
--   
--   Mutually exclusive with <a>defaultsTo</a>. Using both causes a
--   compile-time error.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Text "databaseUrl"
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
required :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Mark a field as containing sensitive data.
--   
--   Secret fields: - Are redacted in <tt>--help</tt> output - Are redacted
--   in error messages - Should use the <tt>Secret</tt> wrapper type for
--   additional protection
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @(Secret Text) "apiKey"
--     |&gt; Config.secret
--     |&gt; Config.required
--   </pre>
secret :: FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a long CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliLong "port"  -- enables --port 8080
--   </pre>
cliLong :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Add a short CLI option flag.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.field @Int "port"
--     |&gt; Config.cliShort <tt>p</tt>  -- enables -p 8080
--   </pre>
cliShort :: Char -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Set environment variable prefix for nested configs.
--   
--   When using nested configs, this prefix is prepended to all nested
--   field environment variable names.
--   
--   <pre>
--   Config.nested @DatabaseConfig "database"
--     |&gt; Config.envPrefix <a>DB_</a>
--   -- Nested fields: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, etc.
--   </pre>
envPrefix :: Text -> FieldDef -> FieldDef

-- | Load configuration from CLI args, environment variables, and config
--   files.
--   
--   This parses configuration at startup and fails fast if any required
--   configuration is missing or invalid.
--   
--   <pre>
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     config &lt;- Config.load @AppConfig
--     let ?config = config
--     runApp
--   </pre>
load :: HasParser config => Task Text config

-- | Load configuration with explicit version and description.
--   
--   The version and description are shown in <tt>--help</tt> output.
--   
--   <pre>
--   main :: IO ()
--   main = do
--     config &lt;- Config.loadWithVersion version "My Application" @MyConfig
--     ...
--   </pre>
loadWithVersion :: HasParser config => Version -> Text -> Task Text config

-- | Get the global config.
--   
--   This function retrieves the application config that was loaded during
--   <a>run</a>. It uses <a>Typeable</a> to recover the concrete type from
--   the type-erased <a>Dynamic</a> storage.
--   
--   <b>IMPORTANT</b>: This function MUST be called after <a>run</a> has
--   loaded the config. Calling it before will result in a runtime error.
--   
--   <pre>
--   myFunction :: Task err ()
--   myFunction = do
--     let port = (Config.get @AppConfig).port
--     Console.print [fmt|Using port: {port}|]
--   </pre>
get :: (Typeable config, HasCallStack) => config

-- | Run an action with a specific configuration for testing.
--   
--   This allows testing code that uses the <tt>HasXxxConfig</tt>
--   constraint without setting up environment variables or CLI arguments.
--   
--   <pre>
--   spec :: Spec
--   spec = describe "OpenRouter integration" do
--     it "sends requests with API key" do
--       let testConfig = def { openRouterKey = Redacted.wrap "test-key" }
--       result &lt;- testWithConfig testConfig do
--         sendToOpenRouter testRequest
--       result.headers `shouldContain` (<a>Authorization</a>, "Bearer test-key")
--   </pre>
testWithConfig :: config -> ((?config :: config) => result) -> result
class HasParser a


module Auth.OAuth2.TokenRefresh

-- | Errors that can occur during token refresh operations.
data TokenRefreshError err

-- | No tokens stored for userId. The Text parameter is the userId (NOT the
--   full key). Example: TokenNotFound "user-123" (not
--   "oauth:provider:user-123")
TokenNotFound :: Text -> TokenRefreshError err

-- | Refresh token is missing from stored token set
RefreshTokenMissing :: TokenRefreshError err

-- | Token refresh request failed
RefreshFailed :: OAuth2Error -> TokenRefreshError err

-- | Storage operation failed
StorageError :: Text -> TokenRefreshError err

-- | Custom action failed (e.g., HTTP request)
ActionFailed :: err -> TokenRefreshError err

-- | Per-key lock acquisition timed out (prevents deadlock). The Text
--   parameter is the lock key (format: "providerName:userId"). SECURITY:
--   Lock key does NOT contain userId alone — it is safe to log.
LockAcquisitionTimeout :: Text -> TokenRefreshError err

-- | Execute an action with automatic token refresh on unauthorized errors.
--   
--   This function: 1. Retrieves tokens from SecretStore using key
--   "oauth:{providerName}:{userId}" 2. Executes the action with the access
--   token 3. If the predicate identifies an "unauthorized" error,
--   refreshes the token 4. Uses per-key locking to prevent duplicate
--   concurrent refreshes (#333) 5. Stores the new TokenSet and retries the
--   action ONCE
--   
--   The refresh action is injectable for testability. In production, pass:
--   <tt>Auth.OAuth2.Client.refreshTokenValidated validatedProvider
--   clientId clientSecret</tt>
--   
--   Example production usage:
--   
--   <pre>
--   withValidToken store refreshLocks "oura" userId
--     (OAuth2.refreshTokenValidated validatedProvider clientId clientSecret)
--     isUnauthorized
--     myAction
--   </pre>
--   
--   Example test usage with mock:
--   
--   <pre>
--   withValidToken mockStore mockLocks "provider" "user1"
--     mockRefreshSuccess
--     isUnauthorized
--     (\token -&gt; Task.yield [fmt|got #{token}|])
--   </pre>
withValidToken :: SecretStore -> ConcurrentMap Text Lock -> Text -> Text -> (RefreshToken -> Task OAuth2Error TokenSet) -> (err -> Bool) -> (Text -> Task err value) -> Task (TokenRefreshError err) value
instance GHC.Classes.Eq err => GHC.Classes.Eq (Auth.OAuth2.TokenRefresh.TokenRefreshError err)
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (Auth.OAuth2.TokenRefresh.TokenRefreshError err)
instance GHC.Show.Show err => GHC.Show.Show (Auth.OAuth2.TokenRefresh.TokenRefreshError err)
